title: Edge Computing for Robotics
coreConcept: A distributed computing paradigm that brings computation and data storage closer to the robots and sensors, reducing latency and bandwidth usage.
theory:
## Edge-to-Cloud Continuum

### Offloading Strategies
Deciding which tasks to run on the robot (e.g., real-time motor control) versus the edge server (e.g., SLAM optimization) or the cloud (e.g., long-term data trends). This is a **Distributed Systems** problem.

### Containerization and Orchestration
Using **Docker** and **Kubernetes (K3s)** to deploy modular robotic microservices. This allows for rapid updates to biotech software without physical intervention.

### Hardware Acceleration
Utilizing FPGAs or TPUs at the edge to run deep learning models for visual inspection of biological samples with sub-millisecond latency.
keyTakeaways: Architect low-latency communication between edge and robot.
Manage containerized workloads in a robotic environment.
Select appropriate hardware accelerators for edge AI.
Implement data reduction techniques at the sensor level.
